notes: Image and audio payloads ARE mirrored into the repo (public/media/, served at /media/...) so the site is offline-capable with no dead links: images converted to WebP and size-optimized, audio served as MP3 for universal browser support. Original source hosts are preserved in each item's pageUrl + verified fields. Only VIDEO stays remote (YouTube embeds / official landing pages), by choice.

Verification method: curl -sI (status + content-type + content-length) for fetchable files; YouTube oEmbed for embed titles/authors; WebSearch/WebFetch where the live host was unreachable from this run.

AUDIO / ATC: No clean official ATC audio file survives on a stable host. archive.org advancedsearch API for 'mh370 atc audio' returned NO recording of the ATC audio — only unrelated 'APG' aviation podcasts and one TEXT item 'audio-transcript-mh-370-pilot-atc-bit' (a Scribd mirror of the transcript, mediatype=texts, not audio). Confirmed the note in spec B.5: archive.org/details/Mh370 is an unrelated book. The only playable copies are third-party YouTube news mirrors.

AUDIO / ATC publisher correction: spec B.5 attributes YouTube CSEbGKiwDn0 to the Guardian, but YouTube oEmbed reports the channel as 'The Telegraph', title 'MH370: listen to final radio communications with air traffic controllers'. Recorded as The Telegraph.

AUDIO / ATC transcript: authoritative transcript is inside the ICAO-hosted preliminary report PDF (spec B.2). Verified live: HTTP 200, application/pdf, 267,847 bytes. Recorded in the atc-radio item's pageUrl companion + notes rather than as its own media item (schema kind is audio|video|image, not document).

HYDROPHONE: found an actual playable candidate-event clip on Wikimedia Commons: 'MH370 Underwater Sound.ogg', released by Curtin University CMST, Public Domain, extracted from a Curtin video. Verified HTTP 200, application/ogg, 66,695 bytes, duration 6.58 s. This is the fetchable clip requested. The Curtin Duncan writeup PDF (HTTP 200, 1,420,677 bytes) and Curtin media release (HTTP 200) are live but contain no standalone .wav/.mp3. 370location.org is live (HTTP 200) but no single stable sample-clip URL was isolable without scraping the SPA; recorded as a reference only, unverified at file level.
VIDEO / GA fly-over: YouTube embed PAJdHx6QT5I verified HTTP 200; oEmbed title 'Overview of the MH370 search area', author 'GeoscienceAustralia'. CC BY 4.0 per spec B.12.

VIDEO / ATSB search videos page (https://www.atsb.gov.au/mh370-search-videos): UNREACHABLE from this run — curl HEAD/GET returned HTTP 000 (connection failed; likely geo/edge or JS gating from this egress) and WebFetch timed out repeatedly. Existence + contents confirmed via WebSearch: Fugro Discovery footage, 'Dragon Prince' towfish, GO Phoenix vessel, and CSIRO drift-modelling animations, all under ATSB Creative Commons (CC BY). Individual per-video URLs could NOT be extracted because the page never loaded here.

VIDEO / Fugro: official contractor underwater-search footage lives in the YouTube playlist PL-iTTUJxPhy1Pzpkaxw_z68Uu6_zdlCex ('The search for MH370'). curl returned HTTP 302 to consent.youtube.com (EU consent wall) so per-video IDs were not enumerated; the playlist URL itself is stable. © Fugro, embed only.

VIDEO / CSIRO drift animations: the griffin directory in spec B.9/B.12 (http://www.marine.csiro.au/~griffin/MH370/) is DEAD — http:// 301-redirects to https:// which returns HTTP 404; no KMZ/mp4 index exists. The CSIRO blog post (blog.csiro.au/...fate-of-flight-mh370) is also gone (301 to csiro.au/en/news). archive.org wayback 'available' API returned no snapshot for either at query time (API may have been throttled). Surviving official host for the drift animations is the ATSB search-videos page; the live CSIRO Bluelink page (research.csiro.au/bluelink/atsb-reports-on-the-search-for-mh370/, HTTP 200) describes them and links onward to ATSB. Recorded the Bluelink page as the drift item's pageUrl.

IMAGES: sourced via commons.wikimedia.org/w/api.php (search + imageinfo with extmetadata). Flaperon photo 'Boeing 777 Wing Flaperon (Part No. 657 BB).jpg' matches the recovered part number (657BB) in data/debris.json; uploader Bruce C. Cooper, marked Public Domain / 'Own work' — likely a reference photo of the 777 flaperon part TYPE (part 657BB) rather than a confirmed image of the actual Reunion piece; flagged as such in its notes. The two 'Debris washed ashore in Mozambique' photos are Australian Government / ATSB, CC BY 4.0.

IMAGES / Mozambique mapping: the two ATSB Mozambique photos correspond to the two 2015–2016 Mozambique fragments (the '676EB' flap support fairing and the 'NO STEP' horizontal stabiliser panel). The filename ordering (1) vs (2) does NOT reliably indicate which photo shows which piece, so both debris featureIds are attached to each photo and the ambiguity is noted.
All direct file URLs (upload.wikimedia.org, ICAO PDF, Curtin PDF) verified live in this run. No URLs were invented; every unverified item states the reason.

items.title: MH370 ATC radio — final communications with air traffic control
items.publisher: The Telegraph (YouTube mirror of the publicly released ATC audio)
items.license: © publisher, embed only — no public-domain/CC file of the ATC audio exists
items.verified: YouTube embed HTTP/2 200 text/html; oEmbed title 'MH370: listen to final radio communications with air traffic controllers', channel 'The Telegraph'. NBC page HTTP/2 200 text/html.
items.notes: No direct playable audio file survives on a stable host (archive.org has no recording — only a text transcript mirror and unrelated podcasts). Authoritative transcript = ICAO-hosted preliminary report PDF, verified HTTP 200 application/pdf 267,847 bytes: https://www.icao.int/sites/default/files/safety/airnavigation/AIG/Documents/Safety-Recommendations-to-ICAO/Final-Reports/MOT_KIKU_9M-MRO_01-2014_preliminary_report.pdf . NBC pageUrl carries both audio and a transcript. Spec B.5 credited the Guardian; actual channel is The Telegraph. Final transmission '17:19:30 Good night Malaysian Three Seven Zero' occurs near IGARI at the WMKK Lumpur Radar handoff.

items.title: Hydroacoustic candidate-event clip (Curtin CMST, HA01 / Perth region)
items.publisher: Curtin University Centre for Marine Science and Technology (CMST)
items.license: Public domain (released by Curtin CMST; via Wikimedia Commons)
items.verified: HTTP/2 200, content-type application/ogg, content-length 66,695; Commons imageinfo duration 6.58 s.
items.notes: Actual playable clip of the candidate acoustic event, extracted from a Curtin CMST video. The signal is TIME-COMPRESSED relative to the real low-frequency event and must be labelled as such (FR-8.1.5). Primary analysis: Duncan (Jun 2014) PDF, verified HTTP 200 1,420,677 bytes: http://cmst.curtin.edu.au/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/2016/03/duncan_sound_clue_in_hunt_for_MH370_jun14.pdf . Curtin media release (HTTP 200): https://www.curtin.edu.au/news/media-release/curtin-researchers-search-acoustic-evidence-mh370/ . Durable spectrograms are in ATSB Operational Search final report Appendices H & I (spec B.3). Independent reference 370location.org is live but exposes no single stable sample-file URL. Mirrored locally as /media/hydrophone-cmst.mp3 (Wikimedia's own MP3 transcode of the public-domain OGG, audio/mpeg 101,286 bytes) so it plays in every browser including Safari, which cannot decode Ogg Vorbis.

items.title: Overview of the MH370 search area (seabed fly-over)
items.verified: YouTube embed HTTP/2 200 text/html; oEmbed title 'Overview of the MH370 search area', channel 'GeoscienceAustralia'. GA data-release page HTTP/2 200 text/html.
items.notes: Bathymetry fly-over of the underwater search area from the GA MH370 Data Release. CC BY 4.0 per spec B.12.

items.title: ATSB MH370 search videos (Fugro underwater footage + CSIRO drift animations)
items.publisher: Australian Transport Safety Bureau (Commonwealth of Australia)
items.verified: UNVERIFIED — host unreachable from this run: curl HEAD/GET returned HTTP 000 and WebFetch timed out (likely geo/edge or JS gating). Existence + contents confirmed via web search.
items.notes: Official landing page for search footage: Fugro Discovery footage, 'Dragon Prince' towfish, GO Phoenix vessel, and CSIRO drift-modelling animations, all ATSB Creative Commons. Individual per-video URLs could not be extracted because the page did not load here — revisit from an unblocked network to enumerate them.

items.title: Fugro — 'The search for MH370' (underwater search footage playlist)
items.verified: PARTIAL — curl HTTP 302 to consent.youtube.com (EU consent wall); playlist ID confirmed via web search. Per-video IDs not enumerated.
items.notes: Official contractor footage of the underwater seabed search (towfish/AUV operations aboard Fugro Discovery and GO Phoenix). Playlist embed uses the videoseries form.

items.title: CSIRO ocean-drift modelling animation (debris drift from the 7th arc)
items.verified: pageUrl CSIRO Bluelink HTTP 200 text/html. Original griffin directory DEAD: http://www.marine.csiro.au/~griffin/MH370/ 301->https->HTTP 404; CSIRO blog post also gone (301 to csiro.au/en/news).
items.notes: Drift animations show blue/black/red dots (leeway 1.2/1.5/1.8%) released along the 7th arc on 8 Mar 2014, white arrows = daily winds; support debris reaching La Reunion. The spec B.9/B.12 direct hosts (griffin KMZ/animation directory and the CSIRO blog) are both dead. Surviving official copies are on the ATSB search-videos page (see atsb-search-videos item); the Bluelink page recorded here describes them and links onward.

items.title: Boeing 777 wing flaperon (part 657BB) — reference photo
items.notes: Depicts a Boeing 777 flaperon of part number 657BB — the SAME part number as the flaperon recovered at Saint-Andre, Reunion (data/debris.json right-flaperon-reunion). Marked Public Domain / 'Own work'; treat as a reference image of the part TYPE, not a verified photo of the actual recovered piece.

items.title: MH370 debris washed ashore in Mozambique (ATSB photo 1)
items.publisher: Australian Government — Australian Transport Safety Bureau (ATSB)
items.notes: One of the two 2015–2016 Mozambique fragments (the '676EB' right flap support fairing and the 'NO STEP' right horizontal stabiliser panel). Filename ordering does not reliably identify which piece is shown, so both debris featureIds are attached.

items.title: MH370 debris washed ashore in Mozambique (ATSB photo 2)
items.publisher: Australian Government — Australian Transport Safety Bureau (ATSB)
items.notes: Second of the two ATSB Mozambique debris photos; see mozambique-debris-photo-1 note on piece-vs-photo ambiguity.

items.title: MH370 recovered debris — compiled overview
items.notes: User-assembled composite of several recovered debris pieces — useful as an Evidence Library overview thumbnail. Provenance of the constituent images is not individually verified; prefer the ATSB-sourced photos above for authoritative single pieces.

items.title: Search operations — P-8A Poseidon crew scanning the water for debris (Mar 2014)
items.notes: Genuine search-operation imagery from the 2014 aerial/surface search phase (US Navy P-8A Poseidon, 20 Mar 2014). Included as illustrative search-ops evidence; attached to the initial Southern Indian Ocean surface search area.
